records.ety: [L. perquisitum, fr. perquisitus, p. p. of perquirere to ask for diligently; per + quaerere to seek. See Per-, and Quest.]

defs: Something gained from a place or employment over and above the ordinary salary or fixed wages for services rendered; -- examples are, a fee allowed by law to an officer for a specific service; the use of a company automobile or other company property.
quotes: The pillage of a place taken by storm was regarded as the perquisite of the soldiers.
The best perquisites of a place are the advantages it gaves a man of doing good.

defs: Things gotten by a man's own industry, or purchased with his own money, as opposed to things which come to him by descent.
